diagrams.title: Data Flow Architecture for Memory-Register-I/O Instructions
diagrams.description: A block diagram showing the three main components (Memory, CPU with Registers, and I/O Devices) and the data flow paths between them, illustrating how instructions move data across these domains.
diagrams.purpose: Shows the physical architecture and data movement pathways that Memory-Register-I/O instructions operate on, helping students understand where data originates and goes during instruction execution.

diagrams.title: Instruction Types Classification
diagrams.description: A hierarchical diagram categorizing the four main types of Memory-Register-I/O instructions: Load (Memory to Register), Store (Register to Memory), Input (I/O to Register), and Output (Register to I/O), with example mnemonics for each.
diagrams.purpose: Provides a clear classification of all Memory-Register-I/O instruction types, making it easy to distinguish between reading from and writing to different storage locations.

diagrams.title: Instruction Execution Cycle for Memory-I/O Operations
diagrams.description: A flowchart or sequence diagram showing the step-by-step execution of a memory or I/O instruction: Fetch, Decode, Execute, and Memory Access phases, with timing indicators for each phase.
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the temporal sequence of operations when executing Memory-Register-I/O instructions, helping students understand the timing and phases involved in data movement.
